name: DS Ontology (Design System)
description: End-to-end design system ontology modelling the three-tier token cascade (Primitive → Semantic → Component), Figma source integration, multi-brand variant support, atomic design patterns, page/template composition, theme mode management, design rule codification, and application skeleton architecture. v3.0.0 adds Action entity, ActionParameterType enum, executesAction relationship, domSelector/stateBinding properties, guard conditions, accessibility hints, and triggersSyncAfter — making every UI action a first-class ontology entity. Adding a new button = adding JSONLD data only, zero JS code changes. Follows 4-tier EMC cascade (PFC → PFI → Product → App) for skeleton inheritance. Bridges to EFS, EMC, PE, and ORG-CONTEXT.

instanceData.description: PFC normative design rules — 45 DR-* DesignRule + 4 ComponentDesignRule instances (v1.3.0: +DR-CONTAINER-002–006, DR-CTX-SWITCH-001–004, DR-CONTEXT-001–003, DR-NODE-LABEL-001, DR-SEMANTIC-001–006)

PFC base application skeleton v2.0.0 — 22 AppZone definitions (with domSelector), 5 NavLayer groups (L1–L4 with L3 split), 26 NavItem actions (with executesAction + stateBinding), 25 ZoneComponent placements, 62 Action entities (~130 entities). Immutable PFC tier consumed by the 4-tier EMC cascade (PFC → PFI → Product → App).

components.DesignSystem: Root container for design system definition with version, namespace, theme support, and config URL
components.PrimitiveToken: Raw design value at base tier (color scales, spacing units, radii, typography)
components.SemanticToken: Intent-based token referencing primitives via W3C alias pattern (text.primary, surface.default)
components.DesignComponent: UI component definition classified by atomic design level
components.BrandVariant: Brand-specific token configuration binding to Figma source (BAIV, W4M, etc.)
components.FigmaSource: Figma file metadata, file key, sync state, and variable collections
components.TokenCategory: Token grouping by type and tier (colors, spacing, surface, button, etc.)
components.DesignPattern: Atomic design level classification (atom, molecule, organism, template, page)
components.PageDefinition: Page-level design composition specifying component slots with resolved token bindings per brand
components.TemplateDefinition: Template-level wireframe specifying structural layout slots without brand-specific resolution
components.DesignRule: Normative design rule (DR-*) governing visual rendering, styling, WCAG compliance — condition/action/severity model
components.ComponentDesignRule: Component-scoped design rule with constraint type/target/value for per-component rendering rules
components.Application: Root application container with app ID, name, version, type (SPA/MPA), cascade tier — entry point for skeleton-driven UI construction
components.AppZone: Spatial UI region (Z1–Z20+) with type (Fixed/Floating/Sliding/Overlay/Conditional), position, width, visibility rules, z-index, domSelector for zone-to-DOM binding
components.NavLayer: Navigation capability group (L1 Main, L2 View, L3 Context/Admin, L4 PFI Custom) hosted in a zone with render ordering
components.NavItem: Individual nav action (button/toggle/dropdown/select/separator/chip) with executesAction reference, stateBinding for data-driven sync, shortcut, visibility condition
components.ZoneComponent: Component placement binding — places a DesignComponent into an AppZone slot with optional token overrides and visibility
components.Action: First-class UI action entity with functionRef (window function), guardCondition/guardMessage, triggersSyncAfter, parameterType (None/String/Select), and accessibilityHint

crossOntology.name: realizesFeature
crossOntology.purpose: DS → EFS: Design components realise EFS feature specifications

crossOntology.name: configuredByInstance
crossOntology.purpose: DS → EMC: PFI instance config determines brand, Figma source, and token file to load

crossOntology.name: configuredByApp
crossOntology.purpose: DS → EMC: Application skeleton tied to PFI instance config for cascade resolution

crossOntology.name: governedByProcess
crossOntology.purpose: DS → PE: Design extraction/validation/deployment governed by PE process

crossOntology.name: ownedByBrand
crossOntology.purpose: DS → ORG-CONTEXT: Design system to organizational brand ownership

versionHistory.changes: Initial design system ontology migrated from v1 Schema.org format to OAA v6.1.0. 10 entities, 13 relationships (10 internal + 3 cross-ontology bridges to EFS, EMC, PE), 8 business rules, 5 enumerations. Three-tier token cascade, Figma MCP integration, multi-brand support, atomic design patterns.

Added pfiInstanceName property to DesignSystem for quick PF-Instance filtering. Added ownedByBrand cross-ontology bridge to ORG-CONTEXT Brand for organization-to-design-system correlation. Added ORG-CONTEXT-ONT import and dependency. 11 internal + 4 cross-ontology relationships.

Added PageDefinition and TemplateDefinition entities for page-level design composition. New relationships: hasPage, instantiatesTemplate, containsComponent. New business rules: BR-DS-009 (template slot mapping), BR-DS-010 (Figma Make validity). 12 entities, 14 internal + 4 cross-ontology relationships.

Added DesignRule and ComponentDesignRule entities for codifying DR-* normative rules as first-class ontology instances. New relationships: hasDesignRule, hasComponentRule, constrainsToken, overriddenByBrand. New enumerations: RuleScope, RuleCategory, ConstraintType. New business rules: DesignRuleMustHaveCondition, ComponentRuleMustTarget, PFCoreRuleNotOverridable. New join pattern JP-DS-005. 15 entities, 15 internal + 4 cross-ontology relationships, 9 enumerations, 12 business rules.

Extended mutability model: semantic tokens may now be PF-Core when classified as structural by the Design Director. Added Container to RuleCategory enumeration. Updated SemanticToken description to reflect PF-Core eligibility. New design rule DR-CONTAINER-001 (Container Surface Immutability). Added container.surface.default PF-Core semantic token and container TokenCategory to all 5 brand instances. New CSS variable --viz-container-surface mapped in ds-loader.js pipeline.

Application skeleton architecture extension. 5 new entities (Application, AppZone, NavLayer, NavItem, ZoneComponent) enabling ontology-driven dynamic UI construction from JSONLD instance data. 4 new enumerations (ZoneType, CascadeTier, NavItemType, AppType). 10 new relationships including 4-tier EMC cascade support (PFC → PFI → Product → App). 3 new business rules (CascadeImmutability, ZonesMustHaveType, NavItemMustHaveAction). 3 new join patterns (JP-DS-006/007/008). PFC base app skeleton instance data with 21 zones, L1–L4 navigation layers, and component placements. 19 entities, 27 internal + 5 cross-ontology relationships (32 total), 14 enumerations, 15 business rules.

Navigation as ontology. New Action entity with functionRef, guardCondition, guardMessage, triggersSyncAfter, accessibilityHint, and parameterType — every UI action is now a first-class ontology entity. New ActionParameterType enumeration (None/String/Select). New executesAction relationship (NavItem → Action). New domSelector property on AppZone (eliminates ZONE_DOM_SELECTORS manual map). New stateBinding property on NavItem (eliminates hardcoded sync logic for toggles, chips, and disabled states). 2 new business rules (BR-DS-016 ActionMustHaveFunctionRef, BR-DS-017 NavItemMustHaveAction). 1 new join pattern (JP-DS-009 NavItem→Action). PFC skeleton v2.0.0 instance data with 62 Action instances, stateBinding on all stateful NavItems, domSelector on all 22 zones. 20 entities, 28 internal + 5 cross-ontology relationships (33 total), 15 enumerations, 17 business rules. Eliminates ACTION_REGISTRY (~100 lines) and ZONE_DOM_SELECTORS (~25 lines) from JS runtime.

notes: DS-ONT provides the structural foundation for systematic UI/UX building from primitives through semantics to components, now extended with application skeleton architecture and navigation-as-ontology for fully ontology-driven dynamic UI construction. v3.0.0 makes every UI action a first-class Action entity — adding a new button requires only JSONLD data, zero JS code changes. Guard conditions, accessibility hints, state bindings, and zone DOM selectors are all modeled in the ontology. Designed for multi-brand PFI resolution via EMC — each PFI product (BAIV, W4M, co-branded) gets its own BrandVariant with Figma source binding. The 4-tier cascade (PFC → PFI → Product → App) allows skeleton inheritance where PFC defines immutable zones/nav and PFI instances add custom layers. Two complementary PE processes: PE-DS-EXTRACT-001 automates Figma MCP token extraction (6 phases, 6 gates, 98% automation); PE-DS-EXTRACT-002 governs hand-authored skeleton JSONLD (4 phases, 4 gates, manual). BAIV instance demonstrates full three-tier token cascade with 15 component tokens. VHF-Viridian instance copied to same-repo instance-data for direct loading.
